novice.title: Candle to Signal
novice.description: Build the foundational vocabulary.  By the end you will read any candlestick, identify a trend, find support/resistance, and interpret your first QuantGlass signal.

intermediate.title: Indicators & Risk
intermediate.description: Go deeper: moving averages, RSI momentum, ATR-based stop sizing, risk:reward arithmetic, and position sizing.  After this module you can calculate every number in a QuantGlass signal yourself.

advanced.title: Engine Deep-Dive
advanced.description: Understand how the signal engine thinks: market regimes, MACD, Bollinger Bands, honest backtesting, and the full confidence_basis derivation.  You will be able to explain *why* any signal was generated.

expert.title: Build & Calibrate
expert.description: Walk-forward OOS validation, empirical Bayes calibration, writing custom indicator and strategy extensions, and synthesizing everything into a disciplined personal trading plan.

tracks.title: Market Foundations
tracks.description: Instruments, exchanges, sessions, order types, spreads, and liquidity — how markets actually work before any chart is read.

tracks.title: Chart Literacy
tracks.description: Read any candlestick chart: candles, trends, support/resistance, and volume.

tracks.title: Your First Signal
tracks.description: Read a QuantGlass signal end-to-end and place your first paper trade.

tracks.title: Indicator Mastery
tracks.description: Every built-in indicator family — moving averages, RSI, MACD, Bollinger, ATR, ADX, Donchian, Keltner — exactly as the engine computes them.

tracks.title: Risk & Position Sizing
tracks.description: R-multiples, ATR-based stops, position sizing, and portfolio heat.

tracks.title: Trade Planning & Journaling
tracks.description: Entries, exits, invalidation, and the discipline of a written plan and journal.

tracks.title: Market Regimes
tracks.description: Trending, ranging, and volatile regimes — and why the same setup behaves differently in each.

tracks.title: Strategy Families
tracks.description: Trend pullback, breakout retest, mean reversion, momentum — tied to the engine's real setup detectors.

tracks.title: Backtesting & Statistical Honesty
tracks.description: In-sample vs out-of-sample, overfitting, walk-forward, expectancy, Sharpe, Sortino, profit factor.

tracks.title: Execution & Costs
tracks.description: Fees, slippage, liquidity, and partial fills — where paper results and live results diverge.

tracks.title: Trading Psychology
tracks.description: Biases, tilt, revenge trading, and the routines that keep a plan intact.

tracks.title: Quantitative Validation
tracks.description: Conformal prediction, calibration, empirical Bayes shrinkage, and Monte Carlo drawdowns.

tracks.title: Building Extensions
tracks.description: Write custom indicators and strategies with the QuantGlass extension SDK.

tracks.title: AI-Assisted Research
tracks.description: Fact-guarded narration, structured outputs, and MCP workflows — using AI without letting it invent numbers.

tracks.title: Going-Live Readiness
tracks.description: Broker plumbing, key custody, and safety gates. Educational only — never financial advice.

tracks.title: Scam Defense
tracks.description: Recognize and refuse the scams that target new traders: fake gurus, signal sellers, phishing, and social-engineering schemes.

tracks.title: Margin, Leverage & Short Selling
tracks.description: Borrowed buying power, liquidation math, short mechanics, and why amplification cuts both ways. Educational only — QuantGlass itself is spot, paper-first.

tracks.title: Options Foundation
tracks.description: Calls, puts, premium, time decay, and the buyer/seller asymmetry — enough to understand options and to know why they are out of engine scope.
